skill.name: gh-cli
skill.description: GitHub CLI (gh) comprehensive reference for repositories, issues, pull requests, Actions, projects, releases, gists, codespaces, organizations, extensions, and all GitHub operations from the command line.
skill.summary: GitHub CLI (gh) comprehensive reference for repositories, issues, pull requests, Actions, projects, ...
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation/reference skill for the GitHub CLI. All static scanner findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation with command examples - no executable code, no credential access, no file system manipulation. The detected patterns (external_commands, network, filesystem, env_access) are all legitimate documentation content showing how to use the gh CLI tool.

medium_findings.title: Static Scanner False Positives - Documentation Skill
medium_findings.description: The static scanner detected 350 potential issues, but all are false positives. This is a markdown documentation file, not executable code. The findings represent: command examples in code blocks (external_commands), GitHub URLs for CLI installation (network), example SSH key paths in docs (filesystem), and environment variable documentation (env_access). No actual security risks present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Extensive manual review confirms this is a pure documentation skill with no executable code, no credential access, and no file system manipulation. All scanner findings are artifacts of documentation content.

content.value_statement: This skill provides comprehensive reference documentation for GitHub CLI (gh), enabling AI assistants to execute GitHub operations from the command line including repositories, issues, pull requests, Actions, and more.
actual_capabilities: Execute gh CLI commands for repository operations (create, clone, fork, view)
Manage GitHub issues (create, list, close, label, assign)
Handle pull requests (create, review, merge, diff, checks)
Work with GitHub Actions (run, workflow, artifact, watch)
Manage GitHub projects, releases, gists, and codespaces
Configure gh CLI settings and authentication
limitations: Requires gh CLI to be installed on the system
Requires GitHub authentication (gh auth login) before use
Cannot perform operations without appropriate GitHub permissions
Depends on network connectivity to GitHub API

use_cases.title: Developer Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Automate common GitHub operations like creating issues, managing PRs, and checking workflow status directly from AI conversations.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers who want to streamline GitHub workflows

use_cases.title: Repository Management
use_cases.description: Create, clone, fork, and manage GitHub repositories using natural language commands through the CLI.

use_cases.title: CI/CD Integration
use_cases.description: Monitor and manage GitHub Actions workflows, check build statuses, and download artifacts via CLI.

prompt_templates.title: Check Repository Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gh-cli skill to show me the status of the current repository including open issues and PRs.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick status check for active repository

prompt_templates.title: Create Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-cli to create a pull request with title 'Add new feature' and description from the file feature.txt.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-cli to list the last 10 open issues labeled 'bug' in the owner/repo repository.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor Workflow Runs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gh-cli to check the status of the latest GitHub Actions workflow run and show any failed jobs.

output_examples.input: Show me the last 5 pull requests
Create an issue titled 'Memory leak in dashboard'
output_examples.output:
Issue created successfully!
https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/456
Title: Memory leak in dashboard
State: open
Created at: 2026-03-13T08:15:00Z

best_practices: Always authenticate with gh auth login before performing operations
Use --json flag for machine-readable output that can be parsed
Specify repository with --repo owner/repo when not in a git repository
Use --web flag to open GitHub web interface when visual confirmation is needed
anti_patterns: Running gh commands without first verifying authentication status
Using personal access tokens in command line (use gh auth login instead)
Ignoring errors from gh commands assuming they succeeded
Not checking rate limits before making bulk API calls

faq.question: How do I authenticate with GitHub using this skill?
faq.answer: Run 'gh auth login' interactively or use 'gh auth login --with-token' with a personal access token.

faq.question: Does this skill require gh CLI to be installed?
faq.answer: Yes, the gh CLI must be installed on your system. Use 'brew install gh' on macOS or your package manager on Linux.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with GitHub Enterprise?
faq.answer: Yes, use 'gh auth login --hostname enterprise.github.com' to authenticate with GitHub Enterprise instances.

faq.question: How do I specify a different repository?
faq.answer: Use the --repo flag: 'gh issue list --repo owner/repo' or set GH_REPO environment variable.

faq.question: Can I get JSON output for parsing?
faq.answer: Yes, use --json flag: 'gh repo view --json name,description' returns machine-readable JSON.

faq.question: How do I check my authentication status?
faq.answer: Run 'gh auth status' to see current authentication state and available scopes.
